one_line: UNH recovery narrative is mid-confirmation: MCR improved to 83.9%, $1.5B AI cost-cut program launched, 2027 MA rates cleared at +2.48%; Q2 earnings Jul 16 is the next inflection gate with open DOJ criminal probe as the unresolved tail risk keeping consensus from turning fully crowded.
catalysts: Q2 2026 earnings Jul 16 pre-open (EPS est $4.84, +18.6% YoY — margin confirmation event); DOJ criminal+civil Medicare Advantage billing probe open with no timeline (binary tail risk); 2027 CMS Medicare Advantage rate finalized +2.48% (cleared Apr 2026, below exec needs but above feared +0.09%); -2.8M MA/Medicaid member exits ongoing through 2026 (right-sizing, revenue headwind before 2027 re-growth); $1.5B AI initiative targeting ~$1B direct opex reduction (Avery assistant, 25% call-center volume cut).

citations.fact: Q2 2026 earnings July 16 before open; diluted EPS est $4.84 (+18.6% YoY from $4.08); 26 analysts cover UNH: 19 Strong Buy, 3 Moderate Buy, 3 Hold, 1 Strong Sell; avg price target $413.16 (~1.8% upside from current); UNH +32.8% over past 52 weeks vs S&P +20.8%.

CMS finalized 2027 Medicare Advantage payment rate increase; UNH shares rallied 9.37% to $307.73 on the announcement, providing sector-wide relief after prior pressure from proposed near-flat +0.09% rate.

UNH at 52-week high (range $234.60-$427.93), up 25% YTD; Q1 2026 revenue $111.7B (+2% YoY), EPS $6.90, MCR 83.9% (down 90bps YoY); $1.5B AI initiative targeting ~$1B direct cost reductions; 17 consecutive annual dividend raises, recently raised 5% to $2.32/quarter.

DOJ criminal and civil investigations into UNH Medicare Advantage billing practices remain open with no resolution timeline; UNH confirmed cooperation with both probes in July 2025; management describes 2026 as 'right-sizing' with intentional exit of 3M+ unprofitable members; first revenue decline in a decade expected for FY2026; Warren Buffett acquired ~5M shares signaling insider value confidence.
